skill.name: lark-okr
skill.description: 飞书 OKR：管理目标与关键结果。查看和编辑 OKR 周期、目标（Objective）、关键结果（Key Result）、对齐关系、量化指标。当用户需要查看或创建 OKR、管理目标和关键结果、查看对齐关系时使用。
skill.summary: 飞书 OKR 管理工具 - 查询和编辑周期、目标、关键结果、对齐关系
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate Lark/Feishu OKR CLI integration tool. The detected patterns (backtick execution, path traversal, hardcoded URLs, certificate/key) are all markdown documentation content showing example commands and JSON structures. No actual security risks present.

content.value_statement: Track and manage OKR cycles, objectives, key results, and alignments with the Lark/Feishu integration. Claude and Claude Code can help you view, create, and update OKR records through the lark-cli tool.
actual_capabilities: List OKR cycles for users with date filtering support
Retrieve detailed objectives and key results for a cycle
Create, update, and delete objectives and key results
Update positions and weights for objectives and key results
Query and modify quantitative indicators for progress tracking
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary to be installed on the system
User can only edit OKR records owned by that user
Cannot access OKR records where owner is a department (team OKR feature)

use_cases.title: Check team OKR status
use_cases.description: Managers can retrieve OKR cycles and objectives for their direct reports to review team progress and alignment.

use_cases.title: Update OKR progress
use_cases.description: Individual contributors can update key result progress indicators to keep their OKR status current.

use_cases.title: View alignment relationships
use_cases.description: Strategy teams can examine how different objectives align across the organization to ensure focus.

prompt_templates.title: View current OKR cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my OKR cycle for this quarter with all objectives and key results

prompt_templates.title: Update key result progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Update my key result [KR name] progress to [percentage] and add a note about current status

prompt_templates.title: Create new objective
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new objective for the current cycle with the title [title] and description [description]

prompt_templates.title: Check alignment connections
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the alignment relationships for my objective [objective name] - what does it support and what supports it

output_examples.input: Show me my OKR cycle for Q1 2024
output_examples.output:
Your Q1 2024 OKR Cycle

Period: Jan 1 - Mar 31, 2024
Status: Active

Objectives:
1. Launch new product feature (80% complete)
   - KR1: Complete development - 90% done
   - KR2: User testing with 50 users - 70% done
   - KR3: Documentation ready - 80% done

2. Improve team collaboration (60% complete)
   - KR1: Weekly sync meetings held - 8/12 completed
   - KR2: Process documentation updated - 50% done

Key Result 'Complete API integration' has been updated.

Current Status:
- Progress: 75%
- Last Updated: 2026-04-26
- Note: Waiting for final testing results

output_examples.input: Create alignment from my objective to the company goal
output_examples.output:
Alignment created successfully.

Your Objective: 'Improve customer satisfaction'
→ Aligns to: Company Goal 'Customer First Initiative'

This shows how your work contributes to the broader company objective.

best_practices: Always read the shared SKILL.md file for authentication setup before using OKR commands
Use the +cycle-list shortcut to quickly find the correct OKR cycle before detailed queries
Include specific objective names or IDs when requesting updates to avoid ambiguity
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to edit OKRs owned by other users - the system will reject these operations
Avoid requesting bulk updates to all objectives without specifying which cycle they belong to
Do not skip reviewing the OKR entity documentation - misunderstanding the relationship between Cycle, Objective, and KeyResult can lead to incorrect operations

faq.question: How do I find my current OKR cycle?
faq.answer: Use the +cycle-list shortcut to get your OKR cycles. The current cycle is typically the one where the date range overlaps with today's date and has status 'normal' or 'default'.

faq.question: Can I edit someone else's OKR?
faq.answer: No, users can only edit OKR records where they are the owner. Department-owned OKRs (team OKR feature) are not editable through this skill.

faq.question: What is the difference between Objective and Key Result?
faq.answer: An Objective (O) is the goal you want to achieve. A Key Result (KR) is a measurable outcome that indicates progress toward that objective. Each objective can have multiple key results.

faq.question: How do alignment relationships work?
faq.answer: Alignments connect objectives so that one objective can support or be supported by another. This creates a hierarchy showing how team objectives contribute to company goals.

faq.question: What does updating weight mean?
faq.answer: Weight determines the relative importance of an objective or key result. When updating weights, all weights in a group must sum to 1.0 (100%).

faq.question: How do I track progress on a Key Result?
faq.answer: Key Results can have quantitative indicators with start, target, and current values. You can update the current_value to track progress against the target.
